summary: 3D Transforms not retained on re-opening of a scene.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in all 4.x (macOS, Windows)
- Reproducible in 3.6.2 (macOS, Windows) - potential more, I just didn't test

### System information

macOS (Tahoe 26.0.1 ), Godot v4.6.dev2.official

### Issue description

3D Transforms not retained on re-opening of a scene.

You will get random rotation results when using different values on  the unlinked scale property.

For the most part his just looks like a bug in the tool inspector, as the values don't change on disk, but also depending on the object sometimes the poly faces will be flipped when you try to change the values to their intended state. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new 3D Scene
2. Select the root node, and edit the transform.  “Unlock Component Ratio” for the Scale property so the values are no longer linked.
3. Set the Scale to be ( -1, 1, 1)

 The  transform should look like:  
```
Position: (0, 0, 0)
Rotation: (0, 0, 0)
Scale: (-1, 1, 1)
```

4. Save the scene, close it, then re-open.   Select the root and inspect the transform, it should now be:  

```
Position: (0, 0, 0)
Rotation: (180, 0, 0)
Scale: (-1, -1, -1)

```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

main.tscn

```
[gd_scene format=3 uid="uid://312n12keaelx"]

[node name="main" type="Node3D" unique_id=986216905]
transform = Transform3D(-1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0)
```

summary: Add missing class registrations

description.text:
* Prepares for #106873

On `master`, when running the godot executable, multiple class registration warnings appear. 
Some of these, which this PR focuses on, are simply a matter of registration order or not being registered at all.

The other two PR focus on the remaining issues:
* #111948
* #111950

With all three PRs combined, I no longer get any warnings when running the engine and landing on the project list. Opening a project still causes ~350 warnings though, which will be the focus of future PRs. 

The changes that are not registration are cascading and required for compilation. Namely:
* Replace `<include>` with `"include"` in `nav_map_2d.h` and `nav_agent_2d.h` - I am not sure why adding the registration causes this to be required, but the compiler said so.
* Added default `StringName()` value to `GDScriptNativeClass`'s constructor. This class needs to be a `GDCLASS` and not a `GDSOFTCLASS` (or it causes a lot of issues), and all registered classes must have a default constructor. 


<details>

Add missing `GodotNavigationServer2D` class registration and necessary include changes for compilation.
Register `GDScriptNativeClass` as internal class.
Call `memnew()` after registering `JavaClassWrapper`.
Remove duplicate registration of `Shader`.
register `NavigationMeshGenerator` before `memnew()`.
remove duplicate `RenderingDevice` registration.
register `Performance` before creating the object.
</details>

summary: BlendTree OneShots need an "abort if interrupted" flag

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in current master, has been in 4 for as long as I can remember. ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8129) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 63.91 GiB memory ### Issue description When a OneShot exists as part of a BlendTree state in a StateMachine, it attempts to fire soon as that state is entered and the OneShot is part of the blending. If that state is exited before the OneShot can complete its duration, then it continues over the course of fading transitions if any exist, and, failing that, pauses. Once the BlendTree is reentered, this OneShot proceeds to resume from wherever it reached. However, this "pausing" behaviour creates situations where the OneShot's purpose is antithetical to... being a one-time shot. If the OneShot is able to resume playback, that means manually having to manage every single OneShot to ensure abortion before reentering a state under different terms. For instance, I have a OneShot in my "crouch" BlendTree state, called "stop." This allows me to have the momentum from moving in the "sneak" state be settled all within the "crouch" idle state, which is at the moment more preferable than using a nested StateMachine to accomplish the same thing. However, if this settle animation is interrupted, without me making all of my transitions out of the "crouch" state long enough to accommodate its end, whenever I return to "crouch", even if under different terms, I am going to be subject to to that "stop" OneShot attempting to finish itself. In this video, I first "sneak" (moving), then enter "crouch" (idle), allowing the "stop" OneShot to reach its conclusion. Then, I move again, and once I stop moving, interrupt the "stop" OneShot to move, then transition into crawl, then come to a stop with my prone idle, then transition from the prone idle into my crouch idle again (also using a OneShot in the crouch state), but the prior "stop" OneShot with the wrong foot still tries to complete itself even though I moved on from it a long time ago. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0b20782d-69e8-4d32-819d-d13d1c8aca6f This was a far bigger problem in Godot 4 pre-#87171 (I think?), when the OneShot would actually restart from the beginning, every time it was interrupted and the state was reentered. I only realized improvements had been made to that recently, but was sticking with my extremely overkill method of ``` for oneshot in crouch_oneshots: animation_tree[oneshot + "/request"] = AnimationNodeOneShot.ONE_SHOT_REQUEST_ABORT ``` from earlier, to be safe. There NEEDS to be a way to force OneShots to always abort if it interrupted midway through. In my opinion, it should be the default. I cannot think of use cases for the current behaviour. If not, a boolean property on the node itself would be welcome. ### Steps to reproduce There's an MRP with an idle state, and an alternate state with a manually armed to fire OneShot. 1. Open MRP. 2. Run game. 3. Observe the idle state of spinning. 4. Hold spacebar to enter the state of spinning in the opposite direction, preceded by a OneShot of the box dropping down. 5. Interrupt this state by releasing spacebar. 6. Return to the state by holding spacebar again. 7. Repeat to observe troublesome behaviour. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [oneshot-interrupt.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23077757/oneshot-interrupt.zip)

summary: Improve validation for adding transition in StateMachine

description.text:
Fixes a couple of errors in StateMachine, that cropped up after #109534:
1. If a new node was created in a fresh StateMachine by shift-dragging out of Start, the editor would print error `INTERNAL ERROR: Index p_transition = -1 is out of bounds (transitions.size() = 0).`
2. When creating a transition by shift-dragging directly out of End or into Start, there was no prevention like what was added for reconnections, so in addition to the error above, we'd also get `INTERNAL ERROR: Condition "p_from == SceneStringNames::get_singleton()->End || p_to == SceneStringNames::get_singleton()->Start" is true.` Popups "Cannot transition from "End"! and "Cannot transition to "Start"! have been added here too, to gracefully stop the creation of invalid transitions.
- Additionally, changes popup "Transition exists!" to "Transition already exists!" to make it a bit clearer, and wraps it in a TTR like the rest.

summary: Mipmaps `limit` is ignored when importing Texture2D

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.5.1 NOT reproducible in 4.4 ### System information Windows 10 ### Issue description When importing to a **Texture2D**, `mipmaps/limit` is absolutely ignored. No matter the value, the importing always behaves as it were set to -1 (that is, no limit). This was not the case in Godot `4.4`. Note that `mipmaps/generate` is still considered. If disabled, no mipmaps will generate appropriately. ### Steps to reproduce - Assuming a new project, select `icon.png` in the **FileSystem** dock. - It can really be any image to be imported as a Texture2D - In the **Import** dock, enable `mipmaps/generate` - In the **Import** dock, set `mipmaps/limit` to a small, arbitrary value greater than `1` - In the **Inspector** dock, look at the resulting image's data in the preview. summary: Two or more external windows freeze game under Wayland+NVIDIA+Vulkan combo

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.3-stable - Reproducible in: 4.4.1-stable - Reproducible in: 4.5.1-stable - Reproducible in: master 9cd297b6f2a0ee660b8f1a6b385582cccf3a9d10 ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable unknown - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sun, 19 Oct 2025 19:21:18 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU - AMD Ryzen 7 7435HS (16 threads) - 15.30 GiB memory ### Issue description Once two or more external (i.e. not embedded) windows are opened, about 7 seconds later all open external windows as well as the game window freeze. I can reproduce this with any mix of Window and FileDialog nodes. This does not reproduce if only one external window is open. The issue occurs both inside the editor as well as in an exported project. Crucially, it does not matter whether the editor itself is using the X11 or Wayland display servers, and it does not matter what display server the game is using. As long as Godot is running under a Wayland compositor, the issue exists. As a side note, I originally noticed this in the texture packer dialog of Terrain3D (a C++ GDExtension). In that case, the editor froze as well (presumably because it is the "game window" in this case). The profiler stops once this happens, consistently at around 6.7 seconds: I can reproduce in both native Wayland mode as well as through XWayland. I'm using KWin 6.5.0 as my Wayland compositor, and I've been able to reproduce on KWin 6.4.5. Confirmed to **not** be reproducible in an X11 session (LXDE, lxdm 0.5.3). Confirmed to be reproducible under Hyprland 0.51.1, although the timing is different (around 17s). summary: ResourceLoader.load with CACHE_MODE_REPLACE_DEEP doesn't refresh all fields

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5-stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8115) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 13.85 GiB memory

### Issue description

Calling 
```gdscript
ResourceLoader.load(FILE_PATH, "Settings", ResourceLoader.CacheMode.CACHE_MODE_REPLACE_DEEP)
```
on a already cached Resource doesn't refresh all properties.
Instead it only refreshes the properties that are mentioned in the resources `.tres` file.





### Steps to reproduce

1. Have this resource:
```gdscript
extends Resource
class_name MyResource

@export var this_refreshes : float = 0.5

@export var this_doesnt : Array[String]
```

2. Load it from this tres file:
```tres
[gd_resource type="Resource" script_class="MyResource" load_steps=2 format=3]

[ext_resource type="Script" path="res://MyResource.gd" id="1_l0ill"]

[resource]
script = ExtResource("1_l0ill")
```

3. Modify the resource instance values to something other than the defaults.

4. Now, reload the resource using `ResourceLoader.load(FILE_PATH, "Settings", ResourceLoader.CacheMode.CACHE_MODE_REPLACE_DEEP)`, and only `this_refreshes` gets correctly reloaded, while `this_doesnt` still is an array

summary: CSG Union fails when multiple hierarchical CSG nodes partially share edges and use the same material

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5.stable, 4.6.dev1

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot 4.5 stable - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700HX (24 CPUs), ~2.1GHz - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060

### Issue description

When performing CSG boolean operations (especially Union), in some case surfaces may merge incorrectly or disappear when each **CSG nodes in a hierarchical tree with four or more nodes partially shares edges and uses the same material in the CSG's Material slot** (including the default material) or when the underlying mesh surfaces use the same material in the case of CSGMesh3D.

**Note**: I am developing a personal plugin that loads brushes from [TrenchBroom](https://trenchbroom.github.io/) into a CSGMesh3D hierarchy tree and bakes them to get the final mesh (not using CSGCombiner3D, because a CSG-level hierarchy handles coplanar faces more reliably).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9241e135-491b-46f0-9222-a1cf5b58d165)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create 4 **CSGBox3D** nodes (all using the default material or same material in the CSG's Material slot).
2. Arrange them in a parent–child hierarchy.
3. Align them so that their edges overlap partially.
4. Set all nodes operation to Union.
5. Observe that some surfaces disappear or merge incorrectly.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f25ed3c-a32f-4980-b800-6924093b0dbc)

summary: Size and shape of specular highlights is unexpected

description.text: ### Tested versions - v4.5.beta.nixpkgs ### System information v4.5.1.stable.nixpkgs [f62fdbde1] ### Issue description The angle and size of specular highlights seems to be kind of weird. It is somewhat visible with "normal" lights ... But it can get really strange if you increase the size of the lamp. Because in general my expectation is that the highlight should look somewhat like the reflection of a sphere - so a sphere. At high roughness values there also is an optical phenomenon where reflections do get very stretched on semi-rough surfaces. My best guess is that this is what the math tries to do but somewhere it went wrong. So this is the setup: the light with a size of 1m (does that mean radius or diameter?) reflected onto a wall and a floor. And while some weirdness is expected when lights intersect a reflecting plane or at very steep angles, the godot behavior is really strange and both eevee and flax do not have these stretching/scaling inconsistencies. | at a low angle | at a high angle | eevee comparison | cycles reference | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | | | | | What is interesting when you compare eevee against cycles is: cycles also slightly has this taper out towards the top of the reflection that godot does. I think the godot implementation is on the right track, but has some math errors that cause it to look very strange as of now. And just to avoid the controversy that messed up the first attempt at me reporting this issue: I am adding the lampshades so you can get a better intuitive understanding of the omnilight/point light size. They are otherwise just lamp shades with an inverted hull that do not interact with the lighting. ### Steps to reproduce 1. add a lamp 2. add a mesh plane 3. move the lamp above the mesh plane 4. add a material to the mesh plane and set its roughness to something lower than 0.4 (makes it easier to see) 5. increase the size of the lamp (makes it easier to see) 6. rotate around the lamp tilting up and down to see if it looks plausible ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) n/a

summary: Forward+: Horizontal NaN lines appear in specular reflections on fully smooth materials when Light3D Size is above `0.0`

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.5.beta1, 4.5.stable, 4.6.dev2 - Not reproducible in: 4.4.stable, 4.5.dev5 ### System information Godot v4.6.dev (c7b176756) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7688) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 63.55 GiB memory ### Issue description Horizontal NaN lines appear in specular reflections on fully smooth materials (`roughness = 0.0`) when Light3D's **Size** (**Angular Distance** for DirectionalLight3D) is above `0.0`: (There are NaNs - if you enable SSIL, the whole scene turns black as a result.) The lines' appearance change according to the mesh subdivision level (a subdivided plane will have shorter lines). All light types are affected. Unlike https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110207, this issue is reproducible in Forward+ but **not** in Mobile due to the specular lobe size not being affected by the Light3D **Size** property in Mobile. Compatibility looks correct. The issue is reproducible both in Vulkan and Direct3D 12. When using Direct3D 12, it's also reproducible with WARP software rendering (`--gpu-index 1`), although the appearance of the lines will be slightly different: The red light on the left has its Size set to `0.0`, while the green light has Size set to `0.5` and the blue light uses `1.0`. This reflection's strength is also not affected by the Light3D Specular property. It completely disappears if Specular is exactly `0.0`, but will appear identical regardless of its value otherwise: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/034c7afd-1fd4-4209-928b-3411c2e9656c ### Steps to reproduce - Add an OmniLight3D, SpotLight3D or DirectionalLight3D at a grazing angle above a floor with `roughness = 0.0`. - Increase the Light3D's Size or Angular Distance property. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [specular-lobe.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23031949/specular-lobe.zip)

summary: Make editor theme previewing faster

description.text:
Doing anything with editor theme is annoying slow. There is a 1.5 Timer. Once it times out, the whole editor freezes for a long time. If you want to check out different colors you have to be quick, if you want to check out different color themes, well...

This PR aims to improve that. The main change is that changing theme no longer applies to the whole editor at once, but for the settings dialog only. The editor updates once dialog is closed.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3864f90b-353f-4e7e-84df-cfc34934a032

I also applied some changes that makes the theme update itself faster:
- Invisible Controls no longer update size on theme change (they do it anyway once made visible)
- The settings' shortcuts tree is only updated when visible.

I managed to reduce the update time quite a bit (the video is taken in a non-optimized dev build), but it still takes a bit, so I added a shorter timer for the dialog update. Otherwise color pickers would be unusable.

There is some bug where the theme does not always apply though.

summary: Wayland: Set window parent before commit

description.text:
See #110503.

Fixes a timing issue where dialogs got configured right before having their parent set. This gave compositors the time to resize/rearrange the dialog as if it were a normal window, only to be marked as "floating" right away. On niri, this manifested as huge dialog windows.

This is achieved with the addition of a `p_parent_id` parameter to `window_create`, akin to its popup counterpart.

`window_create` now also accepts a single `Size2i` parameter instead of two integers, in line with the rest of the `WaylandThread` API. The original idea was to have a very "barebones" API, akin to the `drivers/` directory, but that didn't pan out.

description.text: - Fixes hovering over a line with IME text causing errors `ERROR: scene/gui/text_edit.cpp:6402 - Condition "p_column > text[p_line].length()" is true. Returning: 0` - Fixes search highlight being offset when there is IME text before it - Fixes minimap not being correct when using IME (wrapped lines were messed up) - Fixes (maybe) https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110316 - I'm not sure if this is the same issue or not - Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110787 since that fixes the mouse over issue - But I don't think it actually fixes () https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110138 since that describes Chinese IME specific issues and does not mention mouse movement. Fixed `get_line_wrap_index_at_column` not accepting column with IME. `get_line_wrapped_text` used `text.get_line_wrap_ranges` which uses `data_buf` for the line wraps, which is uses IME text. There are lots more issues with IME here, anything using the TextServer or `data_buf` is using the text with the IME, but some functions use those together with things that don't use the IME text, like the carets, selections, and text strings. Such as `get_rect_at_line_column`, `get_word`, most places that use `get_line_column_at_pos`, and more. Search highlight issue (with an open find panel):

```sh
[nix-shell:~]$ ./a.out 
T: 154058
TZ: EDT
bias_buf: -0400
bias: -240
[nix-shell:~]$ timedatectl 
          Local time: Mon 2025-10-20 15:41:00 EDT
          Universal time: Mon 2025-10-20 19:41:00 UTC
          RTC time: Mon 2025-10-20 19:41:00
          Time zone: America/New_York (EDT, -0400)
          System clock synchronized: yes
          NTP service: active
          RTC in local TZ: no
```

Attach a script to a node - in my case I just used a Label - with the following code:
```gdscript
extends Label

func _init() -> void:
  var timezoneDict = Time.get_time_zone_from_system()
```

### Issue description

When trying to get the local time from the system using `Time.get_time_string_from_system()`, the returned value is always in UTC instead of my local timezone (EDT in this case):

I looked through the code and found that GDscript is calling `OS_Unix::get_datetime()`, so I copied the contents of that function to a local c++ file and ran it in my command line, getting the following results: (note: I'm also testing timezones in this code, and am simply writing the results to console instead of placing them in a Dictionary, but I was testing if the issue was with c++'s `localtime_r` or with Godot)

summary: specular highlights of low roughness surfaces break conservation of energy for big light sources

description.text: ### Tested versions v4.5.beta.nixpkgs Note: I only tested this in 4.5 but I have noticed oddities that now seem plausible for a very long time. ### System information nixos-unstable, kde wayland, tested in forward + and compatibility ### Issue description Specular reflections of light sources with a size become very bright on surfaces with low roughness the bigger the size is. As a side-note: big light sources do not reflect at all on surfaces with 0 roughness. I think in practice it is mostly a non-issue, but in my intuition it feels also somewhat wrong. ## Direct comparisons These are identical scenes with the brightness of the lights matched using the wall in the background as a visual aid. Godot: The same scene in blender-eevee (ray tracing disabled) Cycles (reflections of the walls have been disabled) ### Disclaimer I made two somewhat related issues that were both quite confusing and created a heated discussion, so I am starting a fresh issue. This is not meant to be an apples to apples comparison, but a demonstration of how the issue I explained in abstraction above can mess with the lighting in a scene. For context: I am using point lights to get better shading on the traffic lights and also mimic the reflections that a bright object would cause. And I am getting pretty close to matching the look actually and I find that quite impressive, but some surfaces just look weird. So try to not directly compare godot against cycles here, but look at how the high roughness surfaces look very similar between cycles and godot and observe how the books or the tv kind of fall apart in godot because they look way too bright. ### TV comparison | Godot | Cycles (somewhat unfair comparison) | | -------- | -------- | | | | ### DVD cases comparison | Godot | Cycles (somewhat unfair comparison) | | -------- | -------- | | | | ### Steps to reproduce 1. open the MRP 2. open energy_test.tscn in godot 3. open blender 4. open energy_test.blend 5. enable render preview in blender and make sure eevee is selected as render engine ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [failed to upload, available via google drive (sorry)](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OeaQ-IxoSDKLY7uPp1hjy6cZL-DHUVJc/view) The project includes a few more lighting comparisons than what is strictly necessary for this issue. summary: Fix Input modifiers released condition

description.text:
- *Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/71671*

## Issue Description

This is an interesting issue, but I think we should discuss about whether we need to deal with it.

Currently, the combination key behavior is as follows:

1. Press and hold `W`, `InputEventKey: keycode=87 (W), mods=none, pressed=true`
2. Press and hold `Shift`, `InputEventKey: keycode=4194325 (Shift), mods=none, pressed=true`
3. `InputEventKey: keycode=87 (W), mods=shift, pressed=true`, please notice there is **mods=shift**
4. Release `Shift`, `InputEventKey: keycode=4194325 (Shift), mods=none, pressed=false`
5. Release `W`, `InputEventKey: keycode=87 (W), mods=none, pressed=false`

Relevant result of `is_input_pressed()` is:

1. W
2. W, Shift
3. W, Shift, Shift+W
4. W, Shift+W
5. none

Because `InputEvent` used `keycode` to decide which `InputEvent` may be pressed, and the modifier of `InputEvent` must cover the whole modifer of predefined action, it can be decided to **pressed**. But when the `keycode` related key released, it doesnot evaluate this modifier, just equals `keycode` will decide to whole combination key released.

Let us see another scenario:

1. Press and hold `Shift`, `InputEventKey: keycode=4194325 (Shift), mods=none, pressed=true`
2. Press and hold `W`, `InputEventKey: keycode=87 (W), mods=shift, pressed=true`, please notice there is **mods=shift**
3. Release `W`, `InputEventKey: keycode=87 (W), mods=shift, pressed=false`, please notice there is **mods=shift**
4. Release `Shift`, `InputEventKey: keycode=4194325 (Shift), mods=none, pressed=false`

Relevant result of `is_input_pressed()` is:

1. Shift
2. W, Shift, Shift+W
4. Shift
5. none

As described above. The pressed condition is the same, the whole modifier have to cover the action. But the `shift` is trivial. The keycode is more important to the combination key when released.

## Solution

We should discuss about whether we should modify the behavior of releasing condition.

I updated to decide whether requested modifier is part of action modifier when key released. It can change this first scenario to better result.

1. W
2. W, Shift
3. W, Shift, Shift+W
4. W
5. none

But I am not sure if this change has any other impact. e.g. it may cause Shift+W combination key triggered multiple-time released events.

It is appreciate for any advice. Thank you.

description.text:
This command already existed, but it was not present in the context menu of scene tabs.
The option is disabled when the scene is not present in the file system, as reloading the scene is not possible in that situation.

This addition is for convenience: when a user wants to reload a specific scene, they can right click the scene tab and then click on the option.

summary: Add Parallax2D Preview

description.text:
This implements KoBeWi's parallax preview addon as a built-in editor plugin.
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/8680

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d472b229-5ea8-4cc3-87e5-7978150566c1

I'd like to get some feedback on any improvements, and there's a few things I couldn't find examples of elsewhere to go off of, so until I can find all the sloppy mistakes, I'm going to leave this as a draft for now.

Questions I'd like answered:

- [x] How do we feel about `Disabled`, `Accurate`, and `Basic` as the modes? _EDIT: Change to `Disabled`, `Centered`, and `TopLeft` for clarity._
- [ ] Should it remain disabled if `ignore_camera_scroll` is enabled?
- [ ] It auto-updates when clicking the toolbar dropdown, but I don't believe that's a feature of project settings (?), is that acceptable?
- [x] Is there a way to have this as an editor setting rather than a project setting? `Parallax2D` needs to access the mode on creation, and scene cannot have editor dependencies. _EDIT: Added as an editor setting._
- [x] Is there a way to avoid adding preview specific code to the node itself? _EDIT: Used an internal group to keep track of the parallax nodes_
- [ ] Should there still be a preview guide box since we only go off of viewport size, since we can't know the current camera's transform?

summary: Image looks rotated in every software but not in Godot

description.text: ### Tested versions - 4.5.1.stable ### System information Windows 11 (build 22631) ### Issue description We can use the default image viewer of Windows 11 to rotate images 90º. The image looks correctly rotated in every other software I open it in. However, only Godot ignores the rotation. Photoshop, browsers, Paint, etc: Godot: I imagine this happens because the image viewer sets some orientation metadata in the file instead of actually rotating pixels, but Godot ignores it. ### Steps to reproduce - Open any image in the Windows image viewer (I'm not sure what it is called on English systems; maybe Photo Viewer..) - `Ctrl + R` to rotate it - It doesn't look rotated when used in Godot ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [rotated_image_2025-10-19_21-54-53.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22993798/rotated_image_2025-10-19_21-54-53.zip)

summary: Editor prints "Invalid custom type script UID: %s. Removing." and deletes a UID on one computer, but not another.  Has to do with .godot/editor/filesystem_cach10 file

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.5 mono.  Also reproducible in 4.5.1 mono.

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6733) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1355U (12 threads) - 15.68 GiB memory

### Issue description

I have an `@tool` script with a `class_name`, so it appears in the "add node" menu.  Its UID is `uid://ugacr7gb8aji`.

On my laptop, opening a scene with that custom node in it causes the editor to print "ERROR: Invalid custom type script UID: uid://ugacr7gb8aji. Removing."  It then deletes the corresponding line from the .tscn file.  This error also occurs if I attempt to add a new instance of said node through the "add node" menu.

This does _not_ happen on my desktop computer(which also happens to be the machine I originally wrote and committed that script on), so the issue must have something to do with something in the `.godot` folder.

After some digging, I've determined the culprit to be `.godot/editor/filesystem_cache10`.  If I delete that file on my laptop, the bug becomes fixed.  That file must have generated incorrectly on my laptop when I pulled my changes from git.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Unzip the MRP and open it in Godot.  Note that the zip file contains the `.godot` folder---that's not an accident; it's necessary for reproducing the bug.
2. In `ReproductionScene.tscn`:
    * Right click on the root node in the tree
    * click "add child node"
    * search for `MinimumSizeMatchesChild`
    * click "Create"
    * **Expected behavior:** It creates the node without issue.
    * **Actual behavior:** It prints "ERROR: Invalid custom type script UID: uid://ugacr7gb8aji. Removing." in the console.  When you save the .tscn file, the relevant line will be missing from it.
3. Close Godot
4. Delete `.godot/editor/filesystem_cache10`
5. Open the project in Godot again
6. Repeat step 2.  This time, it will work as expected.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[FastDragon_ReproduceInvalidUid_ReducingDotGodotFolder.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22993184/FastDragon_ReproduceInvalidUid_ReducingDotGodotFolder.zip)
